label: Beijing officials defend Jimmy Lai sentencing as decision against foreign interference
label_zh: 北京官員就黎智英判刑辯護，稱這是反對外國干預的裁決

timeline.title: Beijing Officials Conclude Two Sessions with Strong Defense of Jimmy Lai’s Landmark Prison Sentence
timeline.title_zh: 北京官員為黎智英20年刑期辯護 法律團隊確認將不上訴
timeline.sub_title: As annual political meetings conclude, officials frame the sentencing as a blow to foreign collusion while legal avenues for the pro-democracy publisher are effectively exhausted.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 在京舉行「兩會」期間，當局將判決定性為反對外國干涉的勝利，同時香港政府亦決定撤銷獨立的欺詐相關法律訴訟。
timeline.summary:
The political and legal saga surrounding media mogul Jimmy Lai has entered a new phase of finality this week as Chinese officials utilized the conclusion of the annual 'Two Sessions' in Beijing to cement the narrative surrounding his 20-year prison sentence. As the National People’s Congress and the Chinese People’s Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC) wrapped up on March 12, senior officials from the Office for Safeguarding National Security characterized the judiciary's handling of the case as 'flawless.' They asserted that the heavy sentence was a necessary and legitimate response to what they described as a sophisticated campaign of foreign interference aimed at destabilizing Hong Kong.

Adding to the sense of a closing chapter, the Hong Kong Department of Justice confirmed on March 12 that it would not pursue further legal action in a separate fraud case against Lai. While the Court of Appeal had previously overturned his fraud conviction related to the lease of his former headquarters, the government’s decision to drop its appeal highlights a shift in focus toward his more severe national security convictions. Despite halting further legal challenges in the fraud matter, a government spokesperson maintained a sharp rhetorical stance, condemning Lai’s past actions as a 'long-term misuse of public facilities' and emphasizing that the acquittal did not alter the objective facts of his conduct.

This week also marked the effective end of the legal road for Lai regarding his landmark national security conviction. His legal team confirmed that they have received definitive instructions not to lodge an appeal against his December conviction or the subsequent 20-year prison sentence for collusion with foreign forces. With the 28-day [ref:9] appeal window now closed, the focus of Lai’s supporters and international observers has shifted from the courtroom to the diplomatic arena. Observers note that without further legal recourse, any possibility of release for the 78-year-old publisher now hinges entirely on political negotiations between Beijing and Western governments.

In Beijing, the symbolic weight of the case was further underscored by the release of a high-level white paper on national security, which explicitly named Lai as a key figure in the perceived threats against Chinese sovereignty. Analysts suggest that the timing of the document, coupled with the rhetoric seen throughout the Two Sessions, indicates that Beijing intends to use the Lai verdict as a permanent warning to both internal dissent and external actors. Officials have repeatedly pushed back against international outcry from the United Kingdom and the United States, labeling such criticism as a direct violation of international law and an attempt to interfere in China’s internal judicial processes.

As the Two Sessions delegates depart the capital, the narrative from the central government remains resolute: the 'One Country, Two Systems' framework has been successfully reinforced through the rigorous application of national security legislation. For the people of Hong Kong and the international community, the conclusion of these legal and political proceedings marks the start of an indefinite period of 'securitization,' where the fate of one of the city’s most prominent pro-democracy voices appears sealed within the mainland’s increasingly robust security architecture.
timeline.summary_zh:
圍繞黎智英的法律及政治風波已達一個決定性的里程碑，北京及香港當局正鞏固其對這位傳媒大亨20年監禁刑期的立場。在過去24小時內，北京「兩會」的中國高官們對高等法院的裁決進行了有力辯護，稱該判決「無懈可擊」，是保護香港免受外國干涉的必要措施。這些發展與黎智英的國內法律挑戰正式結束同時發生，其辯護團隊證實他不會就其國家安全定罪尋求上訴。

2026年3月11日，黎智英的法律代表通知傳媒，他們已收到「清晰明確的指示」，不就20年刑期提出上訴。黎智英，這名現已關閉的《蘋果日報》78歲創辦人，被判串謀勾結外國勢力及發布煽動性材料罪成。法律分析人士認為，放棄上訴的決定是對現有框架下進一步訴訟將徒勞無功的一種認可。此舉實際上關閉了在香港的司法補救途徑，並將焦點完全轉向潛在的外交談判或人道呼籲以釋放他。[ref:8, 4, 5, 7, 1]

為法律格局增添新層面，香港政府周三確認，不會就先前的一宗詐騙案對黎智英採取進一步法律行動。儘管律政司最初就《蘋果日報》總部租賃違規行為獲得了定罪，但該裁決最近被上訴法院撤銷。政府發言人表示，雖然他們繼續譴責黎智英濫用公帑的行為，但在考慮所有相關因素後，已決定暫停在此特定事項上的進一步法律挑戰。這一決定使得國家安全判決成為黎智英獲釋的唯一剩餘法律障礙。[ref:6]

在北京，國家安全辦公室副主任孫慶業在正在舉行的政治會議間隙讚揚了司法機構。他聲稱法官的裁決完全基於刑事行為，不應受到外國制裁的恐嚇。孫強調，此次判決對那些企圖在香港發動「顏色革命」的人構成了強有力的威懾。他對《國家安全法》和新頒布的《第23條》立法的結合，為未來幾年城市的穩定提供了「強有力的」保障，表示完全有信心。[ref:3]

儘管北京態度強硬，國際上的批評聲浪卻日益加劇。美國和英國已重申呼籲，以人道理由立即無條件釋放黎智英，並引用了他的年齡和據報的健康問題。預計外交壓力將在3月底達到頂峰，屆時美國總統唐納德·特朗普預計將訪問北京。黎智英的家人公開敦促西方領導人將其釋放作為改善與中國關係的先決條件，並強調此案是該地區爭取新聞自由和法治鬥爭的象徵。[ref:2, 4, 1]

timeline.title: Jimmy Lai Decides Against Appeal of 20-Year Sentence as Beijing Officials Praise Flawless Judicial Decision
timeline.title_zh: 黎智英決定不上訴20年刑期 北京官員讚賞完美司法裁決
timeline.sub_title: The pro-democracy media mogul will not challenge his landmark national security conviction, shifting the focus to high-level diplomatic negotiations as Beijing reasserts the ruling's validity against foreign interference.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 民主派傳媒大亨將不挑戰其里程碑式的國安定罪，將焦點轉向高級別外交談判，北京重申裁決對抗外國干預的有效性。
timeline.summary:
In a decisive turn for Hong Kong’s legal landscape, the legal team for Jimmy Lai confirmed on Wednesday that the 78-year-old media tycoon will not appeal his 20-year prison sentence. The decision marks the conclusion of a years-long legal saga for the founder of the now-defunct Apple Daily, who was convicted last December on charges of conspiracy to collude with foreign forces and conspiracy to publish seditious materials. By [ref:4, 2, 1] opting out of the appellate process, Lai has effectively exhausted his domestic legal remedies, shifting the focus of his case from the courtroom to the realm of international diplomacy.

The development coincides with the ongoing "Two Sessions" political gathering in Beijing, where high-ranking officials have doubled down on the severity of the sentence. Sun Qingye, the deputy head of the Office for Safeguarding National Security, described the judicial decision as "flawless" and a necessary measure to protect the city from external meddling. Officials argued that the trial was conducted with full transparency and that the 20-year term—the longest yet under the 2020 National Security Law—reflects the gravity of Lai’s attempts to solicit foreign sanctions against Chinese and Hong Kong authorities.

International observers and human rights organizations have met the news with renewed concern, characterizing the lack of an appeal as a sign of the immense pressure facing the city’s pro-democracy figures. The United Nations and several Western governments, including the United Kingdom and the United States, had previously called for the verdict to be quashed, asserting it was incompatible with international human rights standards. Lai’s family has expressed fear that the sentence effectively amounts to a life term given his advanced age and reports of deteriorating health in solitary confinement.

With the legal proceedings now finalized, attention has turned to the upcoming visit of U.S. President Donald Trump to Beijing, scheduled for late March. Trump has previously signaled his intent to raise Lai’s case directly with President Xi Jinping, suggesting that a political resolution may be the only remaining path for the activist’s release. However, Beijing has consistently rejected such calls, issuing "solemn representations" to foreign governments and insisting that the case is an internal matter governed strictly by the rule of law.
timeline.summary_zh:
在香港法律格局的決定性轉折中，黎智英的法律團隊週三確認，這位78歲的傳媒大亨將不會就其20年監禁刑期提出上訴。此判決標誌著已停運的《蘋果日報》創辦人結束了長達數年的法律訴訟，他去年12月被判犯有串謀勾結外國勢力及串謀發布煽動性材料罪。通過放棄上訴程序，黎智英實際上已窮盡其國內法律救濟途徑，將其案件的焦點從法庭轉移到國際外交領域。[ref:4, 2, 1]

這一發展與北京正在進行的「兩會」政治集會同時發生，高級官員們不斷強調刑期的嚴重性。國家安全辦公室副主任孫青野稱該司法裁決「完美無缺」，是保護城市免受外部干擾的必要措施。官員們辯稱，審判進行得完全透明，而20年刑期——這是2020年《國家安全法》下最長的刑期——反映了黎智英試圖尋求外國制裁中國和香港當局的嚴重性。

國際觀察家和人權組織對此消息表示新的擔憂，認為缺乏上訴是該市民主派人士面臨巨大壓力的跡象。聯合國和包括英國、美國在內的幾個西方國家此前曾呼籲撤銷該裁決，聲稱其不符合國際人權標準。黎智英的家人擔心，鑑於他年事已高且有報導稱其在單獨監禁期間健康狀況惡化，該刑期實際上相當於終身監禁。

隨著法律程序的最終確定，人們的注意力已轉向美國總統唐納德·特朗普預計於3月下旬對北京的訪問。特朗普此前已表示打算直接與習近平主席討論黎智英的案件，暗示政治解決可能是活動人士獲釋的唯一途徑。然而，北京一貫拒絕此類呼籲，向外國政府發出「嚴正交涉」，並堅稱該案件是嚴格按照法治處理的內部事務。

timeline.title: Beijing Officials at Two Sessions Defend Jimmy Lai Sentencing as a Victory Against Foreign Interference
timeline.title_zh: 北京官員在「兩會」期間為黎智英判刑辯護，稱其為反外國干預的勝利
timeline.sub_title: Following the recent decision by Jimmy Lai not to appeal his twenty-year prison sentence, Beijing officials are hailing the verdict as a flawless victory against foreign political interference.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 在黎智英選擇不上訴其二十年監禁判決後，北京官員將此判決譽為對外國政治干預的完美勝利。
timeline.summary:
Beijing’s top officials utilized the ongoing "Two Sessions" political gathering in the capital to issue a resounding defense of the 20-year prison sentence handed to media mogul Jimmy Lai. Sun Qingye, a senior national security official, characterized the High Court’s ruling as flawless and a landmark achievement in the city’s efforts to purge foreign interference. This narrative has gained significant momentum over the past 24 hours as the Chinese leadership seeks to project a unified front regarding the city's legal stability and the uncompromising enforcement of the National Security Law.

During high-level meetings of the National People’s Congress, the discourse has shifted toward framing the conclusion of Lai’s legal saga as an essential step for Hong Kong’s future prosperity. Sun Qingye emphasized that the judiciary acted strictly in accordance with the law, dismissing international calls for sanctions against the presiding judges as malicious and unfounded. These remarks serve [ref:2] to signal to both local and international audiences that the central government maintains total confidence in the judicial process, which it claims has successfully neutralized a primary mastermind of anti-China activities.

The defense of the sentencing comes at a critical juncture, following the confirmation that Lai will not appeal his conviction for conspiracy to collude with foreign forces. Pro-Beijing legal analysts have spent the last day amplifying this development, interpreting the decision as a de facto acceptance of the evidence or a strategic realization that no viable grounds for appeal exist. This has fueled widespread speculation that the focus is now shifting toward political and diplomatic maneuvers, particularly ahead of a highly anticipated visit to Beijing by U.S. President Donald Trump later this month. 

While the primary focus remains on Lai, the legal repercussions of the case continue to unfold for his co-defendants. On Monday, Fung Wai-kong, a former senior editor at the now-defunct Apple Daily, filed a formal appeal against his own 10-year prison sentence. This move highlights a divergence in legal strategy among the defendants and ensures that the broader foreign collusion case will remain under judicial scrutiny even as Lai’s personal legal battles appear to have reached their conclusion. Meanwhile, international human rights organizations and the United Nations continue to decry the 20-year term as a devastating blow to media freedom, citing Lai’s fragile health and advanced age.

Looking forward, Beijing’s assertive stance at the Two Sessions suggests that national security will remain the primary lens through which Hong Kong is governed. The rhetoric from Premier Li Qiang and other leaders indicates that the central government expects the city to leverage this security-first environment to integrate more deeply into national development goals. As the diplomatic community [ref:4, 5] prepares for upcoming high-stakes summits, the case of Jimmy Lai stands as a central point of friction, with Beijing signaling that it views the matter as a settled domestic outcome immune to external negotiation.
timeline.summary_zh:
在北京舉行的「兩會」政治集會期間，北京高官們發表了響亮的聲明，為媒體大亨黎智英被判處 20 年監禁進行辯護。國家安全高級官員孫慶業稱，高等法院的裁決是完美的，並且是該市淨化外國干預努力的里程碑式成就。在過去 24 小時內，隨著中國領導層試圖就該市的法律穩定性和對《國家安全法》毫不妥協的執行力度展現統一戰線，這一敘事獲得了強勁動力。
在全國人民代表大會的高級會議上，討論已轉向將黎智英法律歷程的結束視為香港未來繁榮的必要步驟。孫慶業強調，司法機關嚴格依法辦事，駁斥了國際社會對審判法官實施制裁的呼籲是惡意且毫無根據的。這些言論旨在向當地和國際觀眾表明，中央政府對聲稱已成功消除反華活動主要策劃者的司法程序充滿信心。
此判決的辯護恰逢關鍵時刻，此前已確認黎智英不會對其串謀勾結外國勢力的定罪提出上訴。親北京的法律分析人士花了一天時間來放大這一發展，將此決定解讀為事實上的證據接受，或是戰略上認識到不存在任何可行的上訴理由。這引發了廣泛猜測，認為焦點現已轉向政治和外交策略，尤其是在美國總統唐納·特朗普本月晚些時候預計訪問北京之前。
雖然主要重點仍在黎智英身上，但此案的法律後果仍在對他的共同被告們展開。週一，已停刊的《蘋果日報》前高級編輯馮偉康對其自己 10 年的監禁判決提出了正式上訴。這一舉動凸顯了被告之間法律策略的分歧，並確保更廣泛的外國勾結案將繼續受到司法審查，即使黎智英的個人法律鬥爭似乎已告一段落。同時，國際人權組織和聯合國繼續譴責這 20 年的刑期是對媒體自由的毀滅性打擊，並提及黎智英虛弱的健康狀況和年事已高。[ref:4, 5]

timeline.title: Jimmy Lai Forgoes Appeal Following 20-Year Sentence as Beijing Officials Laud Judicial Outcome
timeline.title_zh: 北京官員肯定黎智英判決，民主派大亨放棄最後上訴選項
timeline.sub_title: The pro-democracy mogul’s legal team confirms the end of his legal battle, while Chinese officials at the 'Two Sessions' praise the ruling as a necessary step against foreign interference.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 中國「兩會」官員讚揚20年刑期是完美防範外國干預的措施，而挑戰這項標誌性定罪的法律窗口已正式關閉。
timeline.summary:
In a significant turn for Hong Kong’s legal and political landscape, the legal team for pro-democracy media mogul Jimmy Lai confirmed on March 9, 2026, that the 78-year-old founder of the defunct Apple Daily will not appeal his conviction or 20-year prison sentence. This decision effectively brings a formal conclusion to a years-long legal saga that has become a global flashpoint for debates over press freedom and judicial independence in the territory. While the legal team provided no specific reason for the move, observers suggest it may signal a shift from legal maneuvers to potential political negotiations aimed at securing his release on humanitarian grounds.

Simultaneously, Beijing officials have doubled down on their defense of the sentencing. Speaking on the sidelines of the ongoing 'Two Sessions' in Beijing, Sun Qingye, deputy head of the Office for Safeguarding National Security, described the ruling as 'flawless.' He asserted [ref:1] that the judges acted in strict accordance with the National Security Law and that the harsh sentence reflected the 'grave nature' of Lai’s crimes, which included conspiracy to collude with foreign forces and publishing seditious materials. Sun further stated that the judiciary should not be intimidated by foreign sanctions, which he dismissed as malicious interference in China’s internal affairs.

International condemnation has reached a fever pitch following the confirmation of the 20-year term. The United Kingdom, the United States, and the European Union have issued scathing rebukes, with UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper characterizing the penalty as 'effectively a life sentence' for the elderly activist. The United Nations [ref:2] High Commissioner for Human Rights, Volker Türk, also called for Lai’s immediate release, arguing that the verdict is incompatible with international human rights standards. These Western powers [ref:3] maintain that the prosecution was politically motivated and designed to silence critics of the Chinese Communist Party.

In Hong Kong and Beijing, the narrative remains focused on national security and the 'rule of law.' Official commentaries from state media like Xinhua have framed the verdict as a victory for justice that serves as a 'stern warning' to anyone seeking to destabilize the region through foreign collusion. Officials emphasized that Lai was the 'mastermind' behind the 2019 unrest and that his actions far exceeded the boundaries of protected speech. They argue that the [ref:2] robust enforcement of national security legislation has returned stability to the city and protected it from external 'anti-China' elements.

As the legal process ends, attention is shifting to the diplomatic arena. Supporters of Lai are looking toward a scheduled visit by U.S. President Donald Trump to China in late March as a possible window for high-level discussions regarding Lai’s status. Although the White House has noted the President's concern for Lai, Beijing has historically maintained that its judicial decisions are sovereign and non-negotiable, leaving the future of the high-profile prisoner uncertain.
timeline.summary_zh:
在香港最受矚目的一場法律戰鬥迎來關鍵性結局之際，北京官員加強了對黎智英20年監禁判決的辯護，因為上訴的正式截止日期已於週一（3月9日）過去。據報導，這家現已停運的《蘋果日報》創始人已指示其法律團隊不就定罪或判決提出上訴，這實際上結束了這場引發國際高度關注、持續多年的法律糾葛的司法階段。北京和香港的當局藉此機會重申他們的立場，即司法程序處理得完全公正，並代表著國家安全的必要保障。

在正在進行的北京「兩會」立法會議期間，國安公署副署長孫慶業將法庭的裁決描述為「完美無瑕」。孫堅稱，法官根據《國家安全法》規定的刑事行為作出了精確的判決，特別針對黎智英被指控的煽動外國制裁和策劃反華活動的角色。他進一步斷言，結果不應受到外國壓力或制裁威脅的影響，並將法律程序定性為一項證明該市法治韌性的內部事務。

中國外交渠道也加強了其反駁西方批評的訊息傳遞。中國駐倫敦大使館嚴厲駁斥了國際媒體的報導，聲稱黎智英是2019年香港騷亂的「主要人物」，而不是外國政府所描繪的「民主捍衛者」。官方聲明強調，黎智英的行為——包括串謀勾結外國勢力——嚴重挑戰了「一國兩制」框架，並為所謂的「嚴重罪行」辯護了最高懲罰。

法律專家認為，放棄上訴的戰略決定可能是為了將焦點從法庭轉移到外交舞台。隨著法律程序的結束，為黎智英獲釋進行潛在的政治談判鋪平了道路。他的家人和支持者 [ref:2] 已公開表示，美國總統唐納·特朗普將於3月下旬訪問北京，這可能成為人道主義討論的關鍵時刻。國際人權組織和一些西方國家繼續呼籲立即釋放黎智英，鑑於他年事已高且健康狀況不佳，經常將漫長的刑期形容為實際上是「死刑」。

判決的最終確定標誌著香港在2020年《國家安全法》下的過渡時期的一個重要里程碑。儘管北京將此案定性為成功遏制外國勢力干預、恢復秩序的努力，但批評者認為，此案預示著該地區獨立媒體和政治異議幾乎被完全壓制。隨著司法程序的塵埃落定，國際社會仍然關注幕後外交是否能為黎智英一案提供解決方案，該案仍然是中國與西方之間的主要摩擦點。
